meta.phase-name: Visual composer builder UX and inline authoring

problem.item: The current composer shipped the first staff-safe pass, but it still feels like a drawer wrapped around CMS controls instead of a true no-code builder.
The top bar is cluttered, draft actions are too text-heavy, and the working-page dropdown currently has a critical layering bug where it can render behind the composer shell.

The preview is still mostly passive. Editors can change data in the drawer, but the core authoring surface does not yet support Softr-style inline text editing, hover affordances, or direct block selection and manipulation.

Structure, content, media, and publish concerns exist, but the interaction model is not yet cohesive enough to feel like one builder. The system needs stronger state synchronization between preview, structure list, draft selection, and page metadata.
Without explicit product guardrails, the composer could drift into either raw CMS forms or uncontrolled styling freedom instead of a constrained block-based builder.

goals.goal: Make the visual composer feel like a preview-first block builder: the page remains the primary surface and the editor behaves like no-code composition rather than a CMS form.
Refactor the composer header into a compact control bar with inline page title, slug, working-page selection, one-click draft creation, visibility state, and route preview access.
Fix the working-page dropdown layering bug across all composer tabs and modal states.
Enable inline preview editing for the MVP text surfaces: headings, paragraph content, and CTA labels, with selection synchronized between preview and structure.
Tighten the structure panel so each block row clearly communicates block type, status badges, and current selection without forcing the user to inspect the page JSON mentally.
Formalize the page and UI state model for multiple drafts, selected block state, preview mode, and tab routing so future composer work has a stable foundation.
Preserve guardrails: blocks remain schema-driven, deeper configuration stays in the sidebar, and the editor does not expose arbitrary styling freedom in this phase.

non-goals.item: Do not turn the composer into a general-purpose style playground with arbitrary typography, spacing, or color overrides.
Do not replace the Payload page document or create a second hidden page store.
Do not add every possible inline editor in one pass. This phase covers high-value text editing first, not every field type.
Do not block phase closure on future slug-uniqueness infrastructure beyond the current validation contract.
Do not expand this phase into a full AI/copilot redesign beyond keeping it compatible with the existing shared-shell approach.

product-rules.item: Core model: page equals an ordered vertical list of reusable blocks, and the editor must preserve that mental model everywhere.
The preview is the primary surface. Users should edit in-context when possible; the sidebar is for deeper configuration and publish controls.
The authoring split remains explicit: `Structure`, `Content`, `Media`, and `Publish` are the four composer surfaces.
Reduce clutter aggressively: remove redundant helper copy, explanatory draft text, and duplicated actions.
Selection must be mirrored. Clicking a block in preview selects it in structure, and selecting a row in structure highlights it in preview.
Performance matters: small edits should not force full-page re-renders when a narrower local update is possible.
Blocks stay schema-bound. Reusable composition is the freedom; arbitrary styling is not.

current-state.item: `src/components/admin-impersonation/PageComposerDrawer.tsx` already ships a four-tab staff-safe composer with page switching, draft save/publish, service-grid editing, and media-slot workflows.
Page creation, slug, visibility, and preview already exist, but the current header layout and helper text still reflect an early-workbench phase rather than a refined builder.
The composer and preview already share page data, but the selected-block interaction model is not yet rich enough for inline editing and direct-manipulation affordances.
The existing shared copilot shell and authoring context should remain compatible with this phase, not be replaced.

builder-shell.item: The top control bar should render in two compact rows: title/slug above, and working-page controls below.
Target order: page title input, slug input; then working-page dropdown, create-draft action, visibility control, and route-preview icon action.
The dropdown must render through a portal or other reliable layering strategy so it always clears the drawer shell, tab panels, and overlays.
Redundant draft explanation copy should be removed once the one-click draft action and explicit publish controls exist.

preview-editing.item: Preview hover affordances should include a visible outline and a compact action bar with add-below, duplicate, and delete.
Clicking supported text should enter inline edit mode; blur or explicit commit updates the block state and keeps the content tab synchronized.
Preview selection and structure selection must share the same selected-block state rather than maintaining separate local notions of focus.

state-model.item: The page model for the composer must treat `title`, `slug`, `visibility`, `status`, and ordered `blocks` as first-class state.
The UI state model should explicitly track selected block id, active tab, preview mode, current page id, and current draft id.
Multiple drafts must be supported: switching drafts swaps the composer state, and creating a draft clones the current working state before switching context.
A Zustand-backed or equivalent editor-state layer is acceptable if it clearly separates persisted page data from transient UI state.

definition-of-done.item: The working-page dropdown reliably renders above the composer shell in all tabs and is clickable.
The header layout matches the compact two-row builder design with title, slug, working-page selection, create-draft, visibility, and route-preview controls.
Redundant draft explanation text is removed from the composer surface.
Inline editing works in preview for supported text fields and stays synchronized with the content tab.
Preview and structure selection are mirrored, and block hover controls expose add-below, duplicate, and delete actions.
Structure rows show block name, block type, and applicable badges such as reusable, hidden, or dynamic.
The page and UI state flow supports current page, current draft, selected block, and preview mode without state drift when switching drafts.
Focused verification passes for composer integration, and build/typecheck remain green.

tests-required.item: Integration coverage for page switching, draft creation, visibility changes, and preview-authoring state synchronization.
Component or integration coverage for inline text editing and selected-block mirroring.
Browser verification for the dropdown layering fix, top-bar controls, and basic inline editing flow on the frontend composer.
`npm.cmd exec -- tsc --noEmit` and the relevant Vitest composer suite must pass before the phase closes.

open-questions.item: Should the working-page selector represent drafts and published versions as one list with status labels, or as grouped options within the same control?
Which inline text fields should use blur-to-save versus explicit commit controls to avoid accidental edits?
Do block hover controls belong in the page viewport edge, the block boundary itself, or a shared overlay layer to minimize layout jitter?

tasks.task: Lock the preview-first builder contract: map the page state model, UI state model, and tab responsibilities so the composer behaves like a constrained no-code builder instead of a CMS form.
Fix the working-page dropdown layering bug and refactor the top control bar into the compact title/slug plus working-page/draft/visibility/preview layout.
Implement the improved draft workflow controls: one-click create-draft, inline title and slug editing, compact visibility control, and route-preview action while removing redundant draft helper text.
Add preview selection and hover affordances: block outline, hover action bar, mirrored selection with structure, and direct add-below/duplicate/delete actions.
Ship MVP inline preview editing for headings, paragraph text, and CTA labels with content-tab synchronization.
Refine the structure tab to show block name, type, reusable/hidden/dynamic badges, and always-mirrored current selection.
Stabilize state and performance with explicit current-page/current-draft handling, preview-mode state, and granular updates that avoid unnecessary full-page re-renders.
Add verification: focused Vitest/integration coverage for draft switching and inline editing plus browser checks for the top-bar and dropdown behavior.
